commands.name: version
commands.summary: Return ChemCore CLI product and protocol versions as JSON.

commands.name: capabilities
commands.summary: Return the machine-readable CLI protocol, commands, formats, and examples.

commands.name: schema
commands.summary: Return machine-readable command, target, and capture schemas.

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Report CLI installation paths, environment, and runtime capabilities.

commands.name: about
commands.summary: Return product metadata, installed entrypoints, packaging notes, and agent guidance.

commands.name: examples
commands.summary: Return ready-to-run JSON command scripts and CLI workflows.

commands.name: guide
commands.summary: Locate the installed agent and detailed CLI guides and optionally include their Markdown content.

commands.name: inspect
commands.summary: Inspect a document and write JSON summary/object/molecule/resource data.

commands.name: targets
commands.summary: List stable capture targets, object ids, molecule indices, node ids, bond ids, and bounds.

commands.example: chemcore-cli capture input.cdxml --target molecule:0 --target object:obj_label --out selection.png --scale 6 --expand-rel 0.15
commands.name: capture
commands.summary: Render a deterministic cropped SVG or high-resolution PNG for an object, molecule, node, bond, all content, or explicit bounds.

commands.example: chemcore-cli context input.cdxml --target molecule:1 --radius 80 --out context.json --capture-out context.png --scale 5 --pretty
commands.name: context
commands.summary: Report nearby objects/components around a target, including bounds, ids, spatial relation, group/link metadata, and optional screenshot.

commands.name: detail
commands.summary: Return one target's detail JSON after targets/context discovery.

commands.name: copy
commands.summary: Copy all content or a target object/molecule/node/bond as a ChemCore Office/OLE clipboard payload.

commands.name: session
commands.summary: Start a long-lived JSONL agent session that keeps one document open for repeated targets, detail, context, capture, execute, and save operations.

commands.name: new
commands.summary: Create a new document, optionally by applying a JSON command script.

commands.example: chemcore-cli run input.cdxml commands.json --out edited.cdxml --results results.json --pretty
commands.name: run
commands.summary: Load a document, execute a JSON command script, and optionally save the edited document.

commands.name: convert
commands.summary: Convert an editable document between ChemCore, CDXML/CDX, SDF, and SVG export formats.

commands.name: export
commands.summary: Alias of convert for export-oriented workflows.

agentGuide.summary: Quick machine-oriented guide for Chemcore CLI discovery, precise capture, context lookup, detail lookup, editing, and Office clipboard workflows.
agentGuide.title: Chemcore Agent Guide

detailedGuide.summary: Detailed command and JSON workflow guide for callers that use chemcore-cli directly.
detailedGuide.title: ChemCore CLI Command Guide

capture.defaultOutput: If --out is omitted, capture writes a PNG into the OS temp chemcore-cli directory and returns output.defaulted=true plus the exact path and a default_output_path warning in the JSON manifest.
capture.expansion: Use --expand/--padding for absolute pt expansion, --expand-left/right/top/bottom for per-side absolute expansion, and --expand-rel or --expand-rel-x/y for relative expansion based on target size.
capture.multiSelect: Multiple targets are cropped by their minimum union bounds. The rendered image includes everything visible inside that box plus requested expansion.
capture.render: Capture manifests include render.mode, render.primitiveCount, and render.targets. These describe how many render primitives and nearby node/bond/object targets were used to produce the crop.
capture.resolution: PNG defaults to --scale 10. Use --scale, --width, or --height for sharper or bounded raster output.
capture.stdout: JSON manifest only; rendered image data is written to --out or the default temp capture path.
capture.verification: Capture manifests include output.verified=true and output.bytes after the rendered file is verified on disk.

commandScript.audit: new/run results are lightweight by default. They include top-level and per-command document hash/revision transitions plus selector-form created/updated/deleted summaries.
commandScript.errorPointers: Execution reports include command index, commandType, document transition, changeSummary, and engine error message.
commandScript.historyPolicy: The CLI does not maintain an undo stack or per-step snapshot history. Agents should maintain history with git, temp files, or their own logs.
commandScript.input: A JSON object command or an array of command objects.
commandScript.snapshots: Per-command after snapshots and the top-level final snapshot are opt-in with --inspect-after <include>. Use --inspect-after none or --no-inspect-after to force no snapshots.
commandScript.stdin: Use '-' for commands.json to read JSON from stdin.

context.description: Returns objects, molecules, nodes, and bonds near a target or multi-target selection. Entries include selector ids, bounds, center/edge distance, direction, overlap flags, selectionBoxRelation, group ancestry, child ids, and link metadata.
context.screenshot: Pass --capture-out <path.svg|path.png> to render the same context bounds. The capture object includes render.mode, render.primitiveCount, and render.targets when a screenshot is written.
context.selectionBox: context.selectionBox.contents lists objects/molecules/nodes/bonds inside the target box. Each item reports selectionBoxRelation=inside or partial and isTarget=true only for explicitly selected targets.

copy.defaultPayload: If --payload is omitted, copy writes the payload JSON into the OS temp chemcore-cli directory and reports payload.defaulted=true, payload.verified=true, payload.bytes, and a default_payload_path warning.
copy.stdout: JSON manifest only; large clipboard payloads are written to a payload file.

detail.description: Returns a single object's, molecule's, node's, or bond's detail JSON. Use targets/context first to discover selectors, then detail to expand one selector.
detail.rawPolicy: By default, detail includes raw JSON for the selected entity. Use --summary-only for ids/bounds/relationship metadata only. Use --include-resource to embed the referenced molecule/text/json resource when inspecting an object.

guide.content: Use --include-content with --out <path> when the caller needs Markdown text inside JSON.
guide.description: Returns installed guide metadata. Use --kind agent for the quick agent guide, --kind detailed for the detailed English CLI guide, or --kind all for both.

jsonOutput.default: Commands that print JSON emit compact single-line JSON unless --pretty is present.
jsonOutput.errors: Error JSON includes error.kind, message, hint, fix, usage, examples, and suggestions. Missing argument errors include fix.action=provide_required_argument and machine-readable missing/expected fields.
jsonOutput.out: When complete output matters, pass --out <path> and read that file instead of relying on a console buffer.
jsonOutput.pretty: --pretty only changes JSON whitespace: compact JSON becomes line-broken and indented. It does not change fields, values, output files, exit code, schema, ordering, or command behavior.
jsonOutput.writeVerification: When the CLI writes a file, it verifies after the write that the target exists, is a regular file, and has the expected or minimum byte size. Verification failures are command errors.

protocol.compatibility: v1 fields are intended to remain backward compatible throughout the 1.0 beta line unless explicitly marked experimental.

session.description: Starts a JSON Lines protocol over stdin/stdout. The process keeps one Engine and parsed ChemCore document in memory until close or exit.
session.historyPolicy: The session does not persist undo history. execute responses report before/after revision and per-command results; callers should maintain history with git, files, or their own log.
session.ready: The first stdout line is a ready event. Send one compact JSON request per line and read one compact JSON response per line.

target.multiSelect: For capture/context, repeat --target, pass --targets <selector;selector>, use selection:<selector;selector>, or in JSONL session pass target/targets as an array. The crop box is the minimum bounds union, matching the GUI selection box.

stdout.largeOutputPolicy: For payloads that may exceed console buffers, pass --out <path> and read that file. capture writes image data to --out or a default temp PNG path and returns a JSON manifest.
stdout.pretty: --pretty only changes JSON whitespace: compact JSON becomes line-broken and indented. It does not change fields, values, output files, exit code, schema, ordering, or command behavior.
stdout.writeVerification: File-writing commands verify the written file before reporting success.
